articles.title: AI Uncovers Over 100 Exoplanets, Including Rare Worlds in the 'Neptunian Desert'
articles.summary: A new AI pipeline called RAVEN sifted through 2.2 million stars from NASA's TESS mission and validated 118 exoplanets, including ultra-short-period planets and worlds in the mysterious 'Neptunian desert.' The study also gives the most precise measurement yet of how common close-in planets are.

articles.title: Scientists Chase Animal Joy: Building a 'Joy-o-Meter' for Apes, Dolphins, and Parrots
articles.summary: After decades of studying negative emotions, researchers are finally tackling animal happiness. A bold project aims to develop a 'joy-o-meter' — a set of tests to measure brief, intense joy across species. Early observations reveal chimps giggle and bonobos have surprising toy preferences.

articles.title: May 2026 Skywatching: Eta Aquarids, a Moon-Venus Pairing, and a Blue Moon
articles.summary: May delivers a trio of celestial events. The Eta Aquarid meteor shower, debris from Halley's Comet, peaks before dawn on May 5–6 with swift, trail-leaving meteors. On May 18, the crescent Moon and Venus shine together after sunset. The month ends with a rare Blue Moon on May 31.
